codeReview.summary: The commit fixes one schema-validation failure on a healthy Rust analyze run, but it does so by hardcoding parser-marker kickoff roles to BACK. That makes parser-only kickoff output schema-valid yet semantically incorrect and violates the kickoff detector's own contract that roles are inferred from spawn positions.

issues.description: Parser-authored kickoff markers now assign every player the role BACK. When a parser kickoff marker cannot be merged with a heuristic kickoff in _merge_kickoff_sources(), analyze will emit schema-valid kickoff data with incorrect per-player roles for every participant. The module documentation and heuristic path both define kickoff roles as GO/CHEAT/WING/BACK inferred from spawn positions, so replacing UNKNOWN with BACK fixes validation by falsifying the output rather than preserving correct semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-parser-worker skill says to write or extend failing tests first for the parser contract being changed, but this worker changed runtime behavior without adding a targeted regression test for parser-marker kickoff role handling or the healthy analyze path that originally failed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session 8f1614e7-212b-43be-a816-09f703f7a84b shows the worker ran existing test suites, discovered the UNKNOWN enum failure from a manual CLI run, edited src/rlcoach/events/kickoffs.py, and reported "No new tests were needed" in the handoff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used the rust-parser-worker skill for a change in src/rlcoach/events/kickoffs.py, outside the skill's stated owned-file scope of Rust/parser/adapter-focused files. That scope boundary may be too narrow for parser-contract features whose first failing surface appears downstream in event shaping.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file .factory/skills/rust-parser-worker/SKILL.md says "Do not edit outside the feature’s owned-file scope" and describes ownership as parsers/rlreplay_rust/, src/rlcoach/parser/, and parser-focused coverage, but commit 758c817 edits src/rlcoach/events/kickoffs.py.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, skill instructions, mission guidance, and commit 758c817. The implementation is not acceptable as shipped: it converts an invalid UNKNOWN kickoff role into BACK for all parser-marker players, which can silently produce incorrect kickoff-role data whenever parser markers are emitted without a heuristic merge match. Review report written for scrutiny triage.
